divergence_notes: range: the draft follows the problem text, 2 <= a_1 < ... < a_t < n (Set.Ico 2 n); both hosted theorems (plby, jayyhk) constrain sequence members to Set.Ioc 0 n, admitting 1 and n and dropping the lower bound 2. Asymptotically immaterial (affects g by O(1)), but a real statement-level divergence.

P: the draft uses FC-house Nat.maxPrimeFac (sSup of prime divisors; junk value 0 at n <= 1); both hosted define P n = (n.primeFactors.max).getD 1 (junk value 1 at n <= 1). The two agree on every m >= 2, which covers the draft's whole sequence domain.

sequence encoding: the draft uses a strictly monotone a : Fin t -> Nat with StrictAnti (fun i => (a i).maxPrimeFac), the FC house shape (cf. FC 1056, 1102); both hosted use l : List Nat with l.IsChain (. < .) and (l.map P).IsChain (. > .). Equivalent data.

g: the draft takes sSup {t | exists a : Fin t -> Nat, ...} in Nat, mirroring the hosted sSup {k | exists l, is_valid_seq n l and l.length = k}; t = 0 (empty sequence) is always achievable, and t is bounded by n, so the sSup is well-defined.

theorem shape: identical to both hosted theorems, (fun n => (g n : R)) =Theta[atTop] fun n => Real.sqrt (n / Real.log n). No answer() elaborator: the solved estimate is stated directly as the Theta result, following FC precedent (erdos_296, erdos_390.variants.theta); neither hosted theorem uses answer either.
scope: Cambie's further question (exists c with g(n) ~ c*sqrt(n/log n)) is quoted in the docstring but not formalized as a variant; it remains open and neither hosted file formalizes it.
